publications.ref: Garcia Lopez V, Plate L. Comparative Interactome Profiling of Nonstructural Protein 3 Across SARS-CoV-2 Variants Emerged During the COVID-19 Pandemic. Viruses. 2025 17(3):447

Characteristics[Organism].evidence: Using affinity purification–mass spectrometry (AP-MS), we identify differential protein interactions in nsp3 caused by mutations found in variants

Characteristics[MaterialType].evidence: Using affinity purification–mass spectrometry (AP-MS), we identify differential protein interactions

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Experiment Type: Bottom-up proteomics, Data-dependent acquisition

Characteristics[Treatment].evidence: we identify differential protein interactions in nsp3 caused by mutations found in variants

Characteristics[OrganismPart].reasoning: AP-MS experiments for protein interactions typically use cultured cells, and anchor confirms cell culture
Characteristics[OrganismPart].needs: Confirmation of specific cell culture conditions in methods section

Characteristics[CellLine].reasoning: AP-MS studies with viral protein expression commonly use these transfectable cell lines
Characteristics[CellLine].needs: Specific cell line identification in methods section

Characteristics[GeneticModification].reasoning: Study investigates multiple nsp3 variants (Alpha, Beta, Delta, Gamma, Kappa, Lambda, Omicron) suggesting transfected constructs
Characteristics[GeneticModification].needs: Details on expression constructs and transfection methods

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a comparative interactome study of SARS-CoV-2 nsp3 variants using AP-MS in human cells with TMT quantification. Multiple viral variants (Alpha, Beta, Delta, Omicron, etc.) were investigated for differential protein interactions. Key unknowns include the specific cell line used and detailed experimental conditions for viral protein expression.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: digesting with 0.5 μg trypsin/Lys-C (Thermo, Waltham, MA, USA) overnight at 37 °C with agitation

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: Proteins were reduced using TCEP for 30 min

Characteristics[Label].evidence: TMTpro 16plex (Thermo) was used to label digested peptides

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: dynamic modifications of Met oxidation (+15.995 Da)

Characteristics[Modification].3.evidence: static modifications of TMTpro (+304.207 Da) at Lys, and N-termini

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: MudPIT columns were prepared as described previously, in triphasic layers of 1.5 cm C18/SCX/C18 resins... Sequential injections of 10 μL were carried out for 0, 10, 20, 40, 60, 80, and 100% buffer C

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: Sequential injections of 10 μL were carried out for 0, 10, 20, 40, 60, 80, and 100% buffer C (500 mM ammonium acetate, 94.9% water, 5% acetonitrile, 0.1% formic acid v/v) with a final injection of 90% buffer C and 10% buffer B

Comment[Separation].evidence: triphasic layers of 1.5 cm C18/SCX/C18 resins

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation details are well-documented: proteins were reduced with TCEP, alkylated with iodoacetamide, and digested with trypsin/Lys-C before TMTpro 16plex labeling. MudPIT fractionation using SCX was performed with 8 fractions on C18/SCX/C18 columns. Five modification types were identified including oxidation, acetylation, TMTpro labeling, and carbamidomethylation. No enrichment, depletion, or synthetic peptide methods were used in this AP-MS study.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: analyzed using an Exploris480 mass spectrometer (Thermo)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: HCD fragmentation using a normalized collision energy of 32 (TMTpro)

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: MS2 spectra were gathered in a data-dependent mode

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: ESI was performed from the tip of the microcapillary column using 2.2 kV for spray voltage

Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: HCD fragmentation using a normalized collision energy of 32 (TMTpro)

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: Each injection had a 130 min gradient

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: with a flow rate of 500 nl/min

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry instrumental parameters are comprehensively documented. The study used an Exploris480 with ESI ionization, HCD fragmentation at collision energy 32, data-dependent acquisition mode, and orbitrap mass analyzer for MS2. Search parameters included 20 ppm precursor tolerance, 0.02 Da fragment tolerance, maximum 2 missed cleavages, with 130 min gradients at 500 nl/min flow rate. All key instrumental and acquisition parameters are now confirmed for SDRF completion.

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: All raw filenames contain clear fraction identifiers with pattern 'salt_concentration+C_fraction_number' (e.g., 0C_1, 10C_2, 20C_3). This corresponds to the MudPIT SCX fractionation method using salt concentrations: 0, 10, 20, 40, 60, 80, and 100% buffer C, with a final injection of 90% buffer C and 10% buffer B.

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: For nsp3.1: 11× GFP, 11× nsp3.1 reference, 8× Omicron 21K, 8× Omicron 21L, 8× Lambda 21G, 7× Kappa 21B, 7× Delta 21J, 12× Gamma 20J, 8× Alpha 20I. For nsp3.2: 13× GFP, 13× nsp3.2 reference, 10× Omicron 21K, 10× Delta 21I, 9× Gamma 20J, 9× Beta 20H, 10× Alpha 20I, 6× P1228L.

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.evidence: Sequential injections of 10 μL were carried out for 0, 10, 20, 40, 60, 80, and 100% buffer C with a final injection of 90% buffer C and 10% buffer B

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: Five individual runs were used for analysis. In total, 80 IP samples were distributed across the 5 mass spectrometry runs for nsp3.1. While 80 IPs were performed for nsp3.2, 6 were omitted because mutation P1228L was not found to relate to a variant mutation (so 74 for nsp3.2).

FactorValue[Bait].evidence: Filename patterns clearly indicate bait proteins: 'nsp31' for nsp3.1, 'nsp32' for nsp3.2, and 'VGL' (likely corresponding to GFP controls). Method confirms FLAG IPs were performed with nsp3.1, nsp3.2, or GFP plasmid DNA.

FactorValue[GeneticModification].evidence: Files with 'var' in filename indicate variant constructs. Method describes variant constructs for Alpha, Beta, Delta, Gamma, Kappa, Lambda, and Omicron variants created using site-directed mutagenesis from Wuhan reference strain.

FactorValue[FractionIdentifier].evidence: Same as Comment[FractionIdentifier] - filenames contain clear fraction identifiers corresponding to MudPIT SCX fractionation steps

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].reasoning: Method provides total replicate numbers per condition but individual file-to-replicate mapping requires detailed sample assignment information not available in the text
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].needs: Sample assignment table or metadata linking individual raw files to specific biological replicates within each condition

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: Raw filename analysis reveals clear MudPIT fractionation patterns (8 fractions using salt concentrations 0C_1 through 100C_7 plus 90C10B_8) and bait protein assignments (nsp3.1, nsp3.2, GFP controls, and variants). Total sample numbers are confirmed: 80 nsp3.1 samples and 74 nsp3.2 samples across 5 MS runs with variable biological replicates per condition (7-13 replicates). Individual biological replicate assignments per file remain unresolved and would require additional metadata mapping.
